name: Igor Stravinsky
abstract: Igor Fyodorovich Stravinsky (17 June [O.S. 5 June] 1882 – 6 April 1971) was a Russian composer and conductor with French (from 1934) and American citizenship (from 1945).

works.t: Concerto for Piano and Wind Instruments
Concerto for Piano and Wind Instruments

concerts.label: Joseph Szigeti, Violin, Claudio Arrau, and Igor Stravinsky

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: my sister also had an impact on me because she had a very eclectic taste as a teenager. I mean most teenagers now would have one style of music in their repertoire. And at this time, like at sixteen years old, she had Barbra Streisand, Johnny Mathis, Igor Stravinsky...
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: he was writing a piece for the Woody Herman band... we learned to play the music, and presented it at Carnegie Hall in 1946... we decided we would record it

about_quotes.speaker: Ford Foundation interview
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...present Igor Stravinsky. The first half of this 90-minute profile on Stravinsky will be a thoughtful documentary on the composer's life.

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: From then on I was a convert and a Stravinsky addict and a classical music addict, as I still am today.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chubby Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Igor Stravinsky came up to the Paramount Theater backstage, to rehearse the Ebony Concerto... We played Carnegie Hall with Walter Hendl playing, and that was such a victory for all of us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chubby Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: One of the first people that I started to listen to was Igor Stravinsky... That thing floored me. It blew my mind... That was when I said, yeah, I’m in love with this music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
